title: Cannabis Stock Comparison: Aurora vs. Canopy vs. Tilray
text: Aurora trades at a significant discount compared to its peers. According to analyst Pablo Zuanic, Aurora appears undervalued compared to competitors Tilray and Canopy Growth, which trade at multiples of 3-4x sales while Aurora's valuation sits at 1.4x annualized sales. Aurora's Strengths: International medical cannabis expansion driving revenue growth. European sales doubled quarter-over-quarter to $20 million. Australian MMJ sales increased from $9.3 million to $15.1 million. EBITDA margins improved significantly year-over-year. The company demonstrated improving profitability with adjusted cash gross margins reaching 68% for medical marijuana products, representing a 5% year-over-year increase. Zuanic notes that despite different business models, operational trends and lower valuation offer potential upside for ACB shares amid sector volatility. However, he cautions that international growth presents challenges, as shipments can be inconsistent, making end-market growth predictions difficult. Aurora's combination of higher-margin international revenue and conservative valuation may position it favorably against Tilray and Canopy, though sector pressures remain significant headwinds.
